message:
test(cli_robot): assert robot-docs surfaces persistent agent-exclusion commands in guide/examples/sources topics

Lock in the discoverability contract for the just-documented
`cass sources agents {list|exclude|include}` feature so the
examples/guide/sources topics can't silently regress back to not
mentioning it.

tests/cli_robot.rs
- `robot_docs_guide_provides_usage_info`: extend the existing
  guide-topic assertion to also require that the human-readable
  guide still points agents at `cass robot-docs sources` for source-
  management flows. Catches regressions where the sources-topic
  pointer gets dropped from the guide header.
- `robot_docs_examples_provides_practical_examples`: require the
  examples topic to document at least one concrete exclusion
  invocation — specifically `cass sources agents exclude openclaw` —
  so the quickest-to-surface docs stay aligned with the README's
  quick-start block.
- New test `robot_docs_sources_documents_agent_exclusions`:
  * Runs `cass --color=never robot-docs sources`.
  * Asserts no ANSI escapes are emitted under --color=never.
  * Requires the output to document excluding a noisy harness
    (`cass sources agents exclude openclaw`), the
    `--keep-indexed-data` flag, and that exclusions apply to
    watch-mode (i.e. "watch mode" appears in the topic text).

Pure test coverage — no production code change. Each assertion
targets one concrete string in the docs so failures point the reader
directly at the missing fragment.
